title: Ref Applied to Subsequent Arguments in Task
body:
```
Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
Verilator 5.029 devel rev v5.028-49-g1c38744fd
```

Hello. I'm getting a strange error when changing the order of ref arguments.

```systemverilog
// ref_order.sv
module tb;

logic clk;
initial begin
    clk = 1;
    forever begin
        clk = !clk;
        if (clk) $display("posedge %0t", $time);
        #1ns;
    end
end

task automatic progress_clock_good(int num_cycles, ref logic clk_in);
    while ((num_cycles--) > 0) @(posedge clk_in);
endtask
task automatic progress_clock_bad(ref logic clk_in, int num_cycles);
    while ((num_cycles--) > 0) @(posedge clk_in);
endtask

initial begin
    progress_clock_good(5, clk);
    // progress_clock_bad(clk, 5); // causes error
    $finish;
end

endmodule
```
```
# without progress_clock_bad()
verilator -f verilator.f
...
./obj_dir/Vref_order
posedge 1000
posedge 3000
posedge 5000
posedge 7000
posedge 9000
- ref_order.sv:24: Verilog $finish
```
```
# with progress_clock_bad() uncommented
verilator -f verilator.f
%Error: ref_order.sv:23:29: Ref argument requires matching types; port 'num_cycles' requires 'int' but connection is 'logic[31:0]'.
                          : ... note: In instance 'tb'
   23 |     progress_clock_bad(clk, 5);  
      |                             ^
```

I won't have time to look into this until October, but I am happy to receive points on where to get started!
